Menu

World Cup Gameweek 1

Kansas City Stadium

# Team News: Argentina vs Algeria injury, suspension list, predicted XIs

By Jonathan O'Shea| 16 Jun 2026 02:00, Last updated: 16 Jun 2026 02:00

© Imago / Photogamma

Defending champions Argentina will enter the stage at World Cup 2026 on Tuesday night, starting their Group J campaign against Algeria in Kansas City.

With captain Lionel Messi set to claim his 200th cap, La Albiceleste are ultimately aiming to become just the third team to win consecutive world titles.

However, both they and the Fennec Foxes are expected to be without a key defender for their group opener. Here, Sports Mole rounds up the latest team news.

---

## ARGENTINA vs. ALGERIA

© Imago / Photogamma

## ARGENTINA

Out: Nicolas Tagliafico (calf)

Doubtful: Emiliano Martinez(finger), Leandro Paredes (hamstring), Nico Gonzalez (muscular)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: E. Martinez; Molina,

news_items.title: Argentina vs Algeria: full update on absentees and available players - Foot Africa
news_items.text:
# Argentina vs Algeria: full update on absentees and available players - Foot Africa

Published: 2026-06-16T15:00:00+00:00
Source: foot-africa.com (foot-africa.com)
Language: en

## Story

Argentina vs Algeria: full update on absentees and available players - Foot Africa

# Argentina vs Algeria: full update on absentees and available players

2026 World Cup: Argentina weakened, Algeria at full strength

Africa Today at 11:00

Mahdi Zakaria LAZRINews Editor

Argentina vs Algeria: full update on absentees and available players

16.06.26 21:00 World Cup

Argentina

-

:

Algeria

-

On the eve of the highly anticipated clash between Argentina and Algeria at the 2026 World Cup, both squads are fine-tuning their final preparations. Between withdrawals, uncertainties, and major returns, here’s the comprehensive update on all the key players ahead of this blockbuster fixture.

Just hours before the showdown between Argentina and Algeria at Kansas City’s Arrowhead Stadium, both head coaches now know almost all the cards in their hands. While the Albiceleste will have to deal with several significant doubts, the Fennecs approach this encounter with an almost full squad.

See also: Argentina

---

Defending champions Argentina begin their 2026 FIFA World Cup slate with a Group J matchup against Algeria in Kansas City on Tuesday.

Led by the legendary Lionel Messi in what is certainly his final World Cup, the Albiceleste are gunning to be the first nation to repeat as World Cup champions in over 60 years. The last team to accomplish such a feat was Brazil in 1962, having won their first title four years prior.

The 38-year-old Messi is chasing the all-time World Cup goal-scoring record in the process, as he sits on 13 career goals at the FIFA tournament, five behind leader Miroslav Klose. Kylian Mbappe, who is 11 years yo

news_items.title: Algeria predicted lineup vs Argentina for FIFA World Cup 2026
news_items.text:
# Algeria predicted lineup vs Argentina for FIFA World Cup 2026

Published: 2026-06-16T17:39:28+05:30
Source: khelnow.com (khelnow.com)
Language: en

## Story

Algeria predicted lineup vs Argentina for FIFA World Cup 2026

# Algeria predicted lineup vs Argentina for FIFA World Cup 2026

By Basil Elias Benoy

Set As Preferred Source

From digital storytelling to touchline reporting, Basil covers sport with insight and energy.

Published at :June 16, 2026 at 5:39 PM

Modified at :June 16, 2026 at 5:39 PM

### Both nations are to clash in the first-ever World Cup meeting.

The Algeria national football team are all set for their return at the grandest stage after 12 years, with the team considered among the most dangerous African heavyweights capable of pulling off a massive tournament upset. They arrive highly motivated to assert their tactical dominance and make a mark in the competition.

They will play their opening fixture against defending champions Argentina at the Kansas City Stadium in Missouri, USA. The nation’s most crucial player will be Riyad Mahrez, regarded by many as one of the greatest African players to grace the sport, with the player set to captain the Desert Foxes

# Team News: Argentina vs Algeria injury, suspension list, predicted XIs

By Jonathan O'Shea| 16 Jun 2026 02:00, Last updated: 16 Jun 2026 02:00

© Imago / Photogamma

Defending champions Argentina will enter the stage at World Cup 2026 on Tuesday night, starting their Group J campaign against Algeria in Kansas City.

With captain Lionel Messi set to claim his 200th cap, La Albiceleste are ultimately aiming to become just the third team to win consecutive world titles.

However, both they and the Fennec Foxes are expected to be without a key defender for their group opener. Here, Sports Mole rounds up the latest team news.

---

## ARGENTINA vs. ALGERIA

© Imago / Photogamma

## ARGENTINA

Out: Nicolas Tagliafico (calf)

Doubtful: Emiliano Martinez(finger), Leandro Paredes (hamstring), Nico Gonzalez (muscular)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: E. Martinez; Molina, "}

# Argentina vs Algeria: full update on absentees and available players

2026 World Cup: Argentina weakened, Algeria at full strength

Africa Today at 11:00

Mahdi Zakaria LAZRINews Editor

On the eve of the highly anticipated clash between Argentina and Algeria at the 2026 World Cup, both squads are fine-tuning their final preparations. Between withdrawals, uncertainties, and major returns, here’s the comprehensive update on all the key players ahead of this blockbuster fixture.

Just hours before the showdown between Argentina and Algeria at Kansas City’s Arrowhead Stadium, both head coaches now know almost all the cards in their hands. While the Albiceleste will have to deal with several significant doubts, the Fennecs approach this encounter with an almost full squad.

Defending champions Argentina begin their 2026 FIFA World Cup slate with a Group J matchup against Algeria in Kansas City on Tuesday.

Led by the legendary Lionel Messi in what is certainly his final World Cup, the Albiceleste are gunning to be the first nation to repeat as World Cup champions in over 60 years. The last team to accomplish such a feat was Brazil in 1962, having won their first title four years prior.

The 38-year-old Messi is chasing the all-time World Cup goal-scoring record in the process, as he sits on 13 career goals at the FIFA tournament, five behind leader Miroslav Klose. Kylian Mbappe, who is 11 years yo"}

## Story

Algeria predicted lineup vs Argentina for FIFA World Cup 2026

# Algeria predicted lineup vs Argentina for FIFA World Cup 2026

By Basil Elias Benoy

Set As Preferred Source

From digital storytelling to touchline reporting, Basil covers sport with insight and energy.

Published at :June 16, 2026 at 5:39 PM

Modified at :June 16, 2026 at 5:39 PM

### Both nations are to clash in the first-ever World Cup meeting.

The Algeria national football team are all set for their return at the grandest stage after 12 years, with the team considered among the most dangerous African heavyweights capable of pulling off a massive tournament upset. They arrive highly motivated to assert their tactical dominance and make a mark in the competition.

They will play their opening fixture against defending champions Argentina at the Kansas City Stadium in Missouri, USA. The nation’s most crucial player will be Riyad Mahrez, regarded by many as one of the greatest African players to grace the sport, with the player set to captain the Desert Foxes"}
